name: Missing Deadline
pattern: A task, ticket, or commitment references a due date that has passed (or is imminent) without any record of completion or follow-up.
evidence_flow: A task/commitment item names a date, and no later item records its completion or extension before that date elapses.

name: Version Conflict
pattern: Two or more documents or messages assert different values for the same fact (a number, status, owner, or decision) without reconciliation.
evidence_flow: One evidence item states a value for a field; a second item states a contradicting value for the same field, and neither supersedes the other.

name: Stale Meeting
pattern: A recurring or scheduled meeting persists even though its agenda, attendees, or driving project is no longer active or relevant.
evidence_flow: A meeting/event item references a project or owner that another item marks as closed, departed, or deprioritized.

name: Missing Record
pattern: An action or decision is referenced as having happened, but the expected artifact, log entry, or confirming record cannot be found.
evidence_flow: One item asserts an action occurred and points to where its record should live; no item provides that record.

name: Risky Dependency
pattern: A deliverable or workflow depends on an external party, system, or input that is flagged as unreliable, delayed, or out of the team's control.
evidence_flow: A plan/task item names a dependency, and another item describes that dependency as blocked, late, or unowned.

name: Owner Ambiguity
pattern: A task or area of responsibility has no clear single owner, or multiple parties each assume someone else holds it.
evidence_flow: A responsibility item lacks a named owner, or two items each delegate the same responsibility to a different (or unnamed) party.
